items.title: Xiaomi 开源 MiMo-V2.5-Pro (1.02T MoE, 强 Agent 编码)
items.summary: 4 月 28 日小米开源 MiMo-V2.5 系列(MIT)。Pro 版 1.02T/42B 激活,混合注意力(128-token 滑窗 : 全局 = 6:1,KV cache ~7×↓),1M 上下文。配 MiMo Code agent harness,厂商基准在 SWE-bench Verified(82 vs 79)/Pro(62 vs 55)/Terminal-Bench 2(73 vs 69)超 Claude Code+Sonnet 4.6。尚无 Ascend 移植。数字均厂商口径,provisional。

items.title: Seed 2.1 Pro 抢先版上线 LMArena (闭源, 编码/Agent)
items.summary: 6 月 19 日 ByteDance 把 Seed-2.1-Pro-Preview 放上 Code Arena 试用:前端编码子榜 1539 Elo、全球第 8,约等于 Claude Opus 4.6。闭源 API、无 Ascend 移植——是标杆而非 NPU 载荷。完整基准随正式版未来几周公布。分析见 Blog → Dispatch 01。

items.title: 新一批开源模型 (6月中): Qwen3 Coder Next · MiniMax M2.7 · Nemotron 3 Ultra
items.summary: 6 月中又一波开放权重发布:Qwen3 Coder Next、MiniMax M2.5/M2.7 Highspeed、NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 等(新闻聚合,基准/细节待官方)。

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 & GLM-5 run on Ascend 910B (vLLM-Ascend)
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend's 2026 support matrix adds DeepSeek-V4 (custom 910B ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for spec decoding) and GLM-5 (W8A8C8 quant); Qwen3.5 + W8A8 across Qwen3/DeepSeek/MiniMax. MiniMax-M3 not yet listed.

items.title: GLM-5.2 open-sourced (MIT, 1M context)
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights now live on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). 753B params (GLM-5.1-class 744B MoE / 40B active), context 200K→1M, max output 131K, coding-first. No official benchmarks at launch; media cites it edging GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding (FrontierSWE/SWE-Marathon) at ~1/6 cost — treat as provisional.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 with MSA sparse attention
items.summary: Open-weight 1M-context model, native multimodal + agentic coding. MSA (blockwise sparse on GQA) cuts attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context. Official report on arXiv.
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (1.6T MoE, 1M context)
items.summary: V4-Pro/Flash with hybrid sparse attention (CSA+HCA), Multi-Token Prediction, and a multi-teacher distillation pipeline over 32T+ tokens. Official tech report PDF on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding).

items.title: Agentic RL: a distinct paradigm (survey)
items.summary: Survey reframing agentic RL vs single-turn RLVR — multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony: variance-controlled off-policy RL
items.summary: Controls heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts to keep async/off-policy GRPO stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT announced (FP8, self-built HBM)
items.summary: Next-gen Ascend with native FP8/MXFP4 and Huawei's own HBM — 950PR (inference) ~Q1 2026, 950DT (training) ~Q4 2026. Opens the door to FP8 RL on Ascend.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.13.0 released
items.summary: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage and performance; the rollout engine RL stacks target on NPU.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.summary: Open-weight frontier model with trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context; RL post-training continues the R1 lineage.

items.title: GDRO-RL: distributionally robust GRPO
items.summary: Rollout-/Prompt-GDRO reweight toward worst-case prompt distributions on top of GRPO for more robust reasoning RL.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL: Distributed RL Post-Training on Ascend NPU
items.summary: Native distributed GRPO-family RL on Ascend, with experiments up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date.

items.title: AsyncFlow: Asynchronous Streaming RL Post-Training
items.summary: An async streaming RL framework targeting the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant to NPU constraints like the missing vLLM sleep mode.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Pure large-scale RL (GRPO + verifiable rewards) elicits emergent reasoning with no SFT (R1-Zero); catalyzed the 2025 RLVR wave.

items.title: GSPO: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
items.summary: Sequence-level importance ratios and clipping fix GRPO instability and notably stabilize MoE RL; contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM RL System at Scale
items.summary: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, and overlong reward shaping; 50 on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at half the steps.

items.title: ProRL: Prolonged RL Expands Reasoning Boundaries
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs (KL control, reference resetting) and shows prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies.

items.title: MiniMax-M1: Test-Time Compute with Lightning Attention (CISPO)
items.summary: Open hybrid-attention reasoner trained with CISPO (clips IS-weights, not tokens); full RL on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: AReaL: Large-Scale Asynchronous RL for Language Reasoning
items.summary: Fully decouples rollout from training with staleness-enhanced PPO for ~2.77x speedup; now ships a stable Ascend branch.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.summary: Trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention matching full attention with large speedups at 64K length.

items.title: The Entropy Mechanism of RL for Reasoning LLMs
items.summary: Explains policy-entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and proposes Clip-Cov / KL-Cov to sustain exploration.

items.title: Pretraining LLMs with NVFP4
items.summary: Large-scale 4-bit (NVFP4) pretraining of a 12B model on 10T tokens matching FP8 — the most ambitious FP4 training run reported.

items.title: GLM-4.5: Agentic, Reasoning, and Coding Foundation Models
items.summary: Hybrid thinking/direct MoE (355B/32B) trained with a difficulty-based RL curriculum on the open-source 'slime' framework.

items.title: OmniReasoner: Thinking with Long Audio-Video via Native Tool Use
items.summary: Long audio-video reasoning is difficult for omnimodal LLMs because the decisive evidence is often sparse, cross-modal, and too expensive to preserve with uniformly high-fidelity inputs. We introduce OmniReasoner, a tool-use post-training framework for Thinking with Long Audio-Video: omni-modal LLMs …

items.title: Off-Context GRPO: Learning to Reason on Hard Problems using Privileged Information
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) improves reasoning in large language models. Yet, typical RLVR approaches fail on difficult problems: when a model cannot generate any correct solutions, it receives \textit{zero} learning signal. Providing privileged guidance during training, su…

items.title: The Price of Reasoning: Cost-Quality Tradeoffs in Reinforcement Learning for Neural Machine Translation
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has been established as a viable paradigm for the post-training of Large Language Models (LLMs), including downstream tasks, such as Neural Machine Translation (NMT). With the latest research indicating that RLVR could be the preferred training m…

items.title: Beyond Score Prediction: LLM-Based Essay Scoring and Feedback Generation via Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have been widely applied to automated essay scoring (AES) and automated feedback generation (AFG). However, existing studies rely primarily on prompt engineering or supervised fine-tuning, while systematic research on reinforcement learning (RL) post-training and automat…

items.title: Adopting Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards for Molecular Generation
items.summary: Leveraging large language models (LLMs) for molecular generation has shown remarkable potential in chemical and drug design. Current methods primarily rely on supervised training or fine-tuning with limited datasets, which are insufficient to capture complex molecular design objectives. While some a…

items.title: DobicVLM: Aligning Chest X-Ray Report Generation with Clinically-Grounded Programmatic Rewards via Group Relative Policy Optimization
items.summary: Medical imaging is a cornerstone of diagnostics, yet automated chest X-ray report generation struggles with structural adherence, anatomical completeness, and semantic faithfulness. We introduce DobicVLM, a vision-language model combining supervised fine-tuning on MedGemma-4B with Group Relative Pol…

items.title: Athena-Brain Technical Report: An Efficient Robot Brain for General Intelligence and Embodied Interactio
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in language understanding, reasoning, and world knowledge. As embodied agents become increasingly capable, there is a growing demand for compact models that can serve as an on-device brain, preserving the broad general intelligen…

items.title: Fishing Out Free Riders: Shapley-Based Reward Attribution for Parallel Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at multi-step reasoning, yet current parallel reasoning approaches often fail to distinguish the contributions of individual reasoning paths. Many paths may be redundant, misleading, or even detrimental, but outcome-level rewards assign uniform reward, leading to a…

items.title: H$^2$SD: Hybrid Hindsight Self-Distillation
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved the reasoning capabilities of large language models on tasks such as mathematical reasoning and code generation. However, most RLVR methods assign a scalar outcome reward to an entire trajectory, resulting in sparse sup…

items.title: Summary of DCASE 2026 Task 5: Audio-Dependent Question Answering
items.summary: DCASE~2026 Task~5 introduces Audio-Dependent Question Answering (ADQA), which tests whether large audio-language models answer from the audio rather than from textual priors. An Audio-Dependency Filtering (ADF) pipeline combines silent-audio probing, per-option perplexity, a large language model (LL…

items.title: ISO: An RLVR-Native Optimization Stack
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is rapidly advancing the reasoning capabilities of language models, yet the optimization layer that converts reward feedback into weight-space updates remains poorly understood. Building on our prior analysis (Zhu et al., 2025), we study this mis…

items.title: Cognitive Dual-Process Planning for Autonomous Driving with Structured Scene Knowledge and Verifiable Reasoning-Action Consistency
items.summary: High-level planning for autonomous driving is a knowledge-intensive engineering decision task that requires accurate scene understanding, timely inference, and internally consistent action selection. Vision-language models (VLMs) can make intermediate reasoning explicit, but their use in deployed pl…

items.title: Reasoning Before Translation: Enhancing Legal Machine Translation with Structured Reasoning
items.summary: Neural machine translation (NMT) in the legal domain is a linguistically and conceptually demanding task, primarily due to the complexity of legal language and the high level of precision it requires. The recent emergence of reasoning-capable language models opens new possibilities for tackling such…

items.title: Intelligent Multi-UAV Navigation in ITNTNs: A Hierarchical LLM Approach
items.summary: The deployment of high-speed Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in 3D aerial highways necessitates robust coordination of physical flight kinematics and multi-tier network handovers. While Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) offers rapid tactical control, it lacks the zero-shot strategic reasoning requir…

items.title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Large language models that generate step-by-step reasoning traces have achieved strong performance on complex tasks, and extending them to long-context settings has emerged as an important frontier. However, we identify a critical failure mode in this regime: \emph{repetitive copying}, where models …

items.title: Where Should Optimizer State Live? Tiered State Allocation for Memory-Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training
items.summary: Optimizer state is the largest single line item in the memory budget of mixture-of-experts (MoE) training: on a 6.78B-parameter MoE language model, AdamW keeps 50.6 GB of first and second moments to update 12.6 GB of bfloat16 weights. We study SkewAdam, an optimizer built on the observation that the…

items.title: HyMCache: A KV Cache Framework for Multi-Turn LLM Serving with CXL-Hybrid Memory
items.summary: Long-context, multi-turn, and agentic LLM workloads increasingly reuse previously processed context, making KV-cache reuse essential for reducing redundant computation. However, this reuse shifts the bottleneck to the memory tier that stores and serves reusable KV states at cluster scale. GPU HBM an…

items.title: C$^2$KV: Compressed and Composable KV Cache Reuse for Efficient LLM Inference
items.summary: Long-context inference is central to modern large language model (LLM) applications such as retrieval-augmented generation and multi-document reasoning. To mitigate the growing inference cost, recent work has explored key-value (KV) cache reuse to reduce redundant prefill computation. However, exist…

items.title: SALT: Salience-Aware Lexical Trie for Long-Context Compression
items.summary: As large language models (LLMs) process increasingly longer prompts, computation and KV-cache memory costs have emerged as major bottlenecks in inference systems. Existing input-level prompt compression methods address this, but rank each sentence by a scalar relevance score, treating the document a…

items.title: ExpertPlex: A High-Goodput Disaggregated Serving System for MoE LLMs with Adaptive Persistent Kernels
items.summary: LLMs scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) parameters for superior intelligence, but massive weights and dynamic computation impede efficient serving. Existing instance-level prefill-decode disaggregation isolates the phases on separate full-model replicas. As MoE weights grow, each instance may span tens …

items.title: MXSens: Sensitivity-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization for Efficient LLM Inference
items.summary: 4-bit quantization enables efficient LLM inference, but suffers from significant accuracy degradation due to outliers. Prior work addresses this problem via data rotation or mixed-precision integer quantization, but often relies on software-managed scaling and frequent dequantization, incurring subs…

items.title: LMEdge: QoS-Aware LLM Inference Orchestration on Edge Clusters
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) services increasingly operate on edge infrastructure, enabling low-latency and privacy-preserving AI services. However, efficiently serving LLM requests across heterogeneous and resource-constrained edge devices require orchestration mechanisms that jointly determine model…

items.title: Robust KV Cache Management for LLM Serving under Output Token Length Uncertainty
items.summary: KV cache memory is a primary bottleneck in modern LLM serving systems deployed on GPU clusters. A fundamental challenge is that the KV cache must be reserved upon request arrival, while the output token length remains unknown until generation completes. Under-reservation triggers preemption -- forci…

items.title: RECON: Benchmarking Agent Memory for Compositional Reasoning over Long Contexts
items.summary: Large language models and LLM-based agents are widely used as personal chat assistants, enterprise copilots, and autonomous workflow agents. In all these applications, memory (the ability to retain, access, and reason over information accumulated over long contexts and multiple interactions) plays a…

items.title: SpecLA: Efficient Speculative Decoding for Linear-Attention Models
items.summary: Linear-attention models replace the growing KV cache with recurrent states, but autoregressive decoding still reads, updates, and writes these states one token at a time. Speculative decoding can reduce this cost by verifying several draft tokens in one target pass, yet existing speculative systems …

items.title: FVAttn: Adaptive Sparse Attention with Runtime Load Balancing for Video Generation
items.summary: Video Diffusion Transformers process long spatio-temporal sequences, making self-attention the main bottleneck in high-resolution video generation. Training-free sparse attention reduces this cost, but adaptive Top-$p$ routing creates uneven per-head workloads under multi-GPU sequence parallelism. T…

items.title: QUADS: Stabilizing NVFP4 Reinforcement Learning for MoE via QUantization-error Alignment across Dual Sides
items.summary: Rollout generation is a major bottleneck in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Large Language Models, motivating low-precision rollout acceleration such as FP8. As an emerging low-precision format, NVFP4 combines fine-grained scaling for accuracy preservation with native W4A4 F…

items.title: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels…
